objective: Ensure generated SDKs are thin, public-contract-only, capability-bootstrap-aware, and safe around retry/preflight behavior.

cases.title: SDK generated only from public contract pack
cases.failureMode: SDK types include internal schemas or unpublicized operations.
when: SDK codegen manifest and generated stubs are compared.
then: All generated SDK calls trace to public operation descriptors and public schema catalog entries.

assertions.statement: Internal schemas are excluded from SDK exports.

cases.title: Application modules import SDK, not internal services
cases.failureMode: Sample apps or generated apps import platform service internals.
given: Reference platform skeleton has apps and packages.
then: Apps only import SDK/public contracts; internal module imports fail conformance.

cases.title: Capability bootstrap precedes operations
cases.failureMode: Agent assumes endpoint support without capability discovery.
then: First runtime call obtains and validates capability manifest before state-affecting or query operations.

assertions.statement: Capability discovery is part of SDK initialization.

cases.title: Retry middleware respects reason-code registry
cases.failureMode: SDK automatically retries approval, evidence, identity, or conflict failures.
then: SDK does not auto-retry and exposes safe next action.

assertions.statement: Non-retryable and human-review codes are not retried.

cases.title: Generated examples use preflight before mutation
cases.failureMode: Golden examples teach direct state-affecting calls.
then: Every high-consequence execute call is preceded by matching preflight/dry-run.

acceptanceCriteria: Generated SDK exposes operation-centric safe paths only.
